_description: Expert-verified plant disease treatment protocols. Source: FAO Plant Production Guidelines (2019). Used by the Deterministic Remedial Engine.

protocols.symptoms: Water-soaked spots on leaves turning dark brown with yellow halos
protocols.chemical_control: Apply copper hydroxide or copper oxychloride (2-3g/L) every 7-10 days.
protocols.cultural_control: Remove infected material. Use drip irrigation. Rotate crops (3-year rotation).
protocols.preventive_measures: Use certified disease-free seeds. Apply hot water seed treatment (50C for 25 min).
protocols.safety_note: Wear gloves and eye protection. Observe 3-7 day pre-harvest interval.
protocols.source: FAO Plant Production and Protection Guidelines, 2019

protocols.symptoms: Dark brown-black lesions with concentric rings (target pattern) on older leaves
protocols.chemical_control: Apply chlorothalonil (2g/L) or mancozeb (2.5g/L) every 7-10 days.
protocols.cultural_control: Remove infected leaves. Mulch to reduce soil splash. Maintain plant spacing.
protocols.preventive_measures: 2-year crop rotation away from solanaceous crops. Use resistant varieties.
protocols.safety_note: Wear PPE. Do not apply chlorothalonil within 7 days of harvest.

protocols.symptoms: Water-soaked greasy lesions on leaves/stems; white fluffy growth on leaf undersides
protocols.chemical_control: Apply metalaxyl+mancozeb (2-3g/L) every 5-7 days. Act IMMEDIATELY.
protocols.cultural_control: Remove ALL infected tissue. Destroy affected plants. Improve air circulation.
protocols.preventive_measures: Use resistant varieties. Monitor cool+humid weather forecasts. Begin preventive spraying.
protocols.safety_note: URGENT: disease spreads within days. Wear full PPE. 7-14 day pre-harvest interval.

protocols.symptoms: Yellow patches on upper leaf; olive-green fuzzy mold on lower surface
protocols.chemical_control: Apply chlorothalonil, mancozeb, or copper fungicides every 7-10 days. Cover leaf undersides.
protocols.cultural_control: Reduce humidity below 85%. Improve ventilation. Remove infected leaves.
protocols.preventive_measures: Use resistant varieties. Maintain day/night temperature differential.
protocols.safety_note: Wear mask and gloves during application.

protocols.symptoms: Small circular spots with dark border and light gray center on lower leaves
protocols.chemical_control: Apply chlorothalonil (2g/L), mancozeb, or copper fungicides every 7-10 days.
protocols.cultural_control: Remove infected lower leaves. Avoid overhead watering. Mulch soil.
protocols.preventive_measures: 2-year crop rotation. Remove solanaceous crop debris after harvest.
protocols.safety_note: Wear PPE. Observe pre-harvest interval per product label.

protocols.symptoms: Fine stippling on leaves; webbing on undersides; bronzing in severe cases
protocols.chemical_control: Apply abamectin (1mL/L) or bifenazate (2mL/L) acaricide. Spray leaf undersides.
protocols.cultural_control: Maintain plant hydration. Introduce predatory mites (Phytoseiulus persimilis).
protocols.preventive_measures: Monitor regularly with magnifying glass. Avoid excess nitrogen.
protocols.safety_note: Do not apply abamectin within 3 days of harvest. Toxic to aquatic organisms.

protocols.symptoms: Brown circular lesions with concentric rings on leaves, stems, and fruit
protocols.chemical_control: Apply azoxystrobin, chlorothalonil, or mancozeb every 7-14 days.
protocols.cultural_control: Remove infected material. Improve air circulation. Stake plants.
protocols.preventive_measures: Avoid planting in fields with disease history. Use disease-free transplants.
protocols.safety_note: Follow fungicide label instructions. Wear PPE during application.

protocols.pathogen: TYLCV — transmitted by whitefly Bemisia tabaci
protocols.symptoms: Upward leaf curling; yellowing of leaf margins; stunted growth; reduced fruit set
protocols.chemical_control: No antiviral treatment. Control whitefly with imidacloprid (1mL/L) or thiamethoxam.
protocols.cultural_control: Remove infected plants IMMEDIATELY. Use yellow sticky traps. Install insect-proof screens.
protocols.preventive_measures: Plant TYLCV-resistant varieties. Use reflective mulches. Establish virus-free transplants.
protocols.safety_note: Infected plants cannot be cured — remove immediately. Imidacloprid toxic to bees — do not apply during flowering.
protocols.source: FAO Plant Virology Manual; CABI TYLCV Datasheet

protocols.pathogen: Tomato mosaic virus (ToMV) — mechanically transmitted, seed-borne
protocols.symptoms: Mosaic pattern (alternating light/dark green) on leaves; leaf distortion; internal fruit browning
protocols.chemical_control: No chemical cure. Disinfect tools with 10% bleach or 70% alcohol between plants.
protocols.cultural_control: Remove and destroy infected plants. Disinfect all tools. Wash hands thoroughly.
protocols.preventive_measures: Use ToMV-resistant varieties. Treat seeds with TSP (10%, 15 min). Use certified virus-free seeds.
protocols.safety_note: TSP is corrosive — wear gloves and eye protection.
protocols.source: FAO Seed Health Guidelines; EPPO PM 7/30

protocols.symptoms: No disease symptoms detected. Plant appears healthy.
protocols.cultural_control: Continue weekly monitoring. Maintain irrigation, fertilization, and spacing.
protocols.preventive_measures: Follow IPM principles. Regular field inspection.

protocols.symptoms: Dark brown lesions with concentric rings on older leaves; premature defoliation
protocols.chemical_control: Apply mancozeb (2.5g/L) or chlorothalonil (2g/L) every 7-10 days.
protocols.cultural_control: Remove infected foliage. Maintain adequate potassium nutrition. Avoid water stress.
protocols.preventive_measures: Certified seed potatoes. 2-3 year crop rotation. Resistant varieties.
protocols.safety_note: Wear gloves and mask. 14-day pre-harvest interval for mancozeb.
protocols.source: FAO Potato Crop Protection Manual; CIP Technical Guidelines

protocols.symptoms: Water-soaked lesions; white growth on leaf undersides; dark tuber lesions; rapid crop collapse
protocols.chemical_control: Apply metalaxyl+mancozeb (3g/L) or dimethomorph+mancozeb every 5-7 days. Act IMMEDIATELY.
protocols.cultural_control: Destroy ALL infected material. Hill-up stems. Harvest promptly after haum destruction.
protocols.preventive_measures: Certified blight-free seed tubers. Resistant varieties. Begin preventive spraying in cool/humid weather.
protocols.safety_note: URGENT: Can destroy entire crop in 1-2 weeks. Full PPE required.
protocols.source: FAO Potato Crop Protection Manual; CIP Late Blight Management

protocols.cultural_control: Monitor regularly. Ensure adequate hilling. Monitor for late blight risk.

protocols.symptoms: Water-soaked spots on leaves turning dark brown with yellow halos; scabby lesions on fruit
protocols.chemical_control: Apply copper hydroxide (2-3g/L) + mancozeb every 7 days.
protocols.cultural_control: Avoid overhead irrigation. Remove infected debris. Stake plants.
protocols.preventive_measures: Certified disease-free transplants. Hot water seed treatment (52C, 30 min). 2-year rotation.
protocols.safety_note: Wear gloves and eye protection. Monitor soil copper accumulation with repeated use.
protocols.source: FAO Integrated Management of Bacterial Spot in Pepper; Jones et al. (1991)

protocols.cultural_control: Monitor weekly. Maintain proper irrigation and nutrition.
protocols.preventive_measures: Use drip irrigation to keep foliage dry. Monitor for bacterial spot after rain.
